mnemonics.title: Procedural vs OOP
mnemonics.content: Procedural: procedures, functions, top-down. OOP: objects, classes, bottom-up

acronyms.usage: OOP is a programming paradigm that focuses on objects and their interactions
API is a set of rules and protocols for building software applications

visualTips.visualization: Think of a class as a blueprint for creating objects. Objects are instances of classes.
Think of inheritance as a way of creating a new class based on an existing class. The new class inherits the properties and methods of the existing class.

analogies.analogy: Think of polymorphism as a person who can speak multiple languages. Just as a person can communicate in different languages, an object can take on multiple forms in OOP.
Think of encapsulation as a safe that protects valuable items. Just as a safe keeps its contents secure, encapsulation hides an object's internal details and exposes only its necessary information.
